metadata.title: Implement Enhanced Error Messages and Debugging

story.iWant: clear, actionable error messages with contextual debugging information
story.soThat: I can quickly identify and resolve issues in my service configurations, dependency injection setup, and runtime failures

task.title: Implement Enhanced DI Container Error Reporting
task.subtask: Create DIError type with contextual fields (missing interface, dependency chain)
Add suggestion engine for common DI registration mistakes
Implement dependency chain tracking in container
Add available registrations lookup for error messages

task.title: Add Service Configuration Validation Framework
task.subtask: Create ConfigValidator interface with validation rules
Implement schema-based validation for service configuration
Add environment variable resolution debugging
Create validation error formatter with field-level detail

task.title: Enhance Framework Runtime Error Context
task.subtask: Create ContextualError wrapper with service metadata
Add operation context tracking (CRUD operation, middleware, etc.)
Implement framework-aware stack trace annotation
Add recovery suggestion system based on error patterns

task.title: Implement Debug Mode and Logging
task.subtask: Add DebugMode configuration flag and environment variable
Implement dependency resolution logging with trace IDs
Add service lifecycle event tracking
Create performance metrics collection for DI operations

task.title: Create Error Classification and Handling
task.subtask: Define error type taxonomy (Configuration, DI, Runtime, Framework)
Implement error classification interfaces
Add error severity levels (Fatal, Warning, Info)
Create error reporting utilities for framework bugs

task.title: Testing and Validation
task.subtask: Write unit tests for all error scenarios
Create integration tests with deliberate configuration errors
Validate error message clarity with developer feedback
Benchmark performance impact of debug mode

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: When DI container fails to resolve dependencies, error messages include: - Missing interface registration details - Available registered interfaces - Dependency chain that failed - Suggested fixes (e.g., "Register MyInterface using container.Register[MyInterface](implementation)")

Framework validates service configurations at startup and provides: - Clear indication of invalid configuration keys - Expected configuration format with examples - Environment variable resolution debugging - Schema validation errors with field-level detail

All framework runtime errors include: - Contextual information about the operation being performed - Service instance information (name, type, dependencies) - Stack trace with framework-specific annotations - Recovery suggestions where applicable

Framework includes debug mode that: - Logs detailed dependency resolution process - Tracks service lifecycle events - Provides performance metrics for DI container operations - Enables verbose validation reporting

Errors are categorized by type: - Configuration errors (startup-time, recoverable) - Dependency injection errors (startup-time, fatal) - Runtime service errors (request-time, recoverable) - Framework bugs (unexpected, reportable)

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: FR21: Framework provides clear error messages for dependency injection failures. FR22: Complete documentation includes testing patterns.

artifact.title: System Architecture
artifact.snippet: Context-aware error handling with structured logging through dependency injection. Service composition requires error context to flow through service hierarchies.

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.snippet: Story 4.2: Implement structured error types (DependencyError, CompositionError), dependency graph visualization, debug mode with detailed tracing.

artifact.reason: Basic framework error structure, needs enhancement with context and debugging information
Existing DI error types that form the foundation for enhanced error reporting
Service composition error handling that needs integration with new debugging system
Logging interface that will be extended with debug mode capabilities
Example of existing validation errors that show current error patterns
DI container integration point where enhanced error reporting will be integrated

story-context.constraints: - Error system must integrate with existing logging infrastructure without breaking changes - Debug mode should have minimal performance impact when disabled (< 1% overhead) - Error messages must be actionable and include specific resolution steps - All error types must implement Go error interface and support error wrapping - Configuration validation must work with existing environment variable patterns - Error classification must be consistent across all framework components - Debug logging must support structured logging with context propagation

interface.name: ErrorHandler
interface.signature: type ErrorHandler interface { HandleError(ctx context.Context, err error) error; WrapError(err error, context string) error }

interface.name: DebugLogger
interface.signature: type DebugLogger interface { LoggerInterface; WithTrace(traceID string) DebugLogger; EnableDebugMode(enabled bool) }

interface.name: ConfigValidator
interface.signature: type ConfigValidator interface { Validate(config interface{}) error; ValidateField(field, value string) error }

tests.standards: Framework uses testify/assert and testify/mock for unit testing with interface-based mocking. Integration tests use real implementations but with test databases. Build tags separate unit tests (//go:build unit) from integration tests (//go:build integration). Error testing validates both error types and error messages for clarity.

ideas.idea: Test DI container error reporting with missing dependencies, invalid registrations, and circular dependencies
Test configuration validation errors with invalid keys, missing environment variables, and schema violations
Test runtime error enhancement with service context, operation tracking, and recovery suggestions
Test debug mode logging with trace IDs, dependency resolution tracing, and performance metrics
Test error classification system with proper categorization and severity levels
Integration tests for complete error handling workflow from error occurrence to debug output
